title: Lisas Rival

jokes.text: As a matter of fact, my refrigerator wasn't running. You spared me quite a bit of spoilage. Thank you, anonymous young man.
jokes.setup: Bart making a prank call expecting to embarrass Skinner
jokes.punchline: Skinner sincerely thanks him for the helpful reminder
jokes.explanation: Perfect Skinner character moment - his earnest, formal response completely deflates Bart's prank attempt

jokes.text: I'm gonna need a bigger drill.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to fix Marge's camera with inappropriate tools
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to Jaws while destroying delicate equipment
jokes.explanation: Good pop culture reference that fits Homer's character, though somewhat predictable

jokes.text: My, these seas are certainly heaving. Well, no more than your bountiful bosom, my lady.
jokes.setup: Romantic TV show playing in background
jokes.punchline: Over-the-top cheesy dialogue
jokes.explanation: Generic parody of romance novel dialogue - functional but uninspired

jokes.text: Lisa, stop blowing my sex. I mean, stop blowing your sax. Your sax. Stop it.
jokes.setup: Homer getting flustered by Lisa's saxophone practice
jokes.punchline: Freudian slip creates awkward double entendre
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer moment - the escalating panic and repeated correction makes it work

jokes.text: Hey! Why, that sounds like Gabriel's trumpet. You know what that means, kids. Judgment Day!
jokes.setup: Lisa playing saxophone outside
jokes.punchline: Ned's religious fervor makes him think it's the apocalypse
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Ned's character - his religious extremism leading to absurd conclusions

jokes.text: My cat's name is Mittens.
jokes.setup: Teacher asking about Columbus, students discussing seriously
jokes.punchline: Ralph's completely unrelated contribution
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ralph moment - complete non-sequitur delivered with innocent sincerity

jokes.text: Ralph, this better not be about your cat.
jokes.setup: Ralph previously mentioned his cat
jokes.punchline: Teacher's weary anticipation of another non-sequitur
jokes.explanation: Nice callback that shows Miss Hoover knows her students too well

jokes.text: I never made Miss Hoover 'yow-wee.'
jokes.setup: Allison impressing the teacher with correct answers
jokes.punchline: Lisa's jealous observation about never getting such praise
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Lisa's competitive nature and wounded pride

jokes.text: Are you hyperventilating? No. I just like to smell my lunch.
jokes.setup: Lisa appearing to breathe heavily into lunch bag
jokes.punchline: Innocent explanation subverts concern about anxiety
jokes.explanation: Nice misdirection that reveals character quirk while defusing tension

jokes.text: Out of the way, brain queen!
jokes.setup: Lisa claiming she has wonderful friends
jokes.punchline: Immediate demonstration that her friends are actually dismissive
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing undercuts Lisa's self-deception about her social status

jokes.text: Me too. Me too.
jokes.setup: Girls discovering they have everything in common
jokes.punchline: Conversation devolves into mindless repetition
jokes.explanation: Captures the awkwardness when people try too hard to connect

jokes.text: Jackknifed sugar truck! Sugar?
jokes.setup: Homer calling out road hazards while teaching Bart to drive
jokes.punchline: His voice completely changes when he spots something he wants
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - his priorities immediately shift from safety to self-interest

jokes.text: If only the sugar were as sweet as you, sir.
jokes.setup: Homer being 'helpful' at accident scene
jokes.punchline: Truck driver's inexplicably romantic gratitude
jokes.explanation: Weird unexpected romantic moment that comes from nowhere

jokes.text: White gold! Texas tea! Sweetener.
jokes.setup: Homer excited about finding sugar
jokes.punchline: Grand proclamations ending with mundane reality
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that deflates with the anticlimactic final term

jokes.text: Read your town charter, boy. If foodstuff should touch the ground, said foodstuff shall be turned over to the village idiot. Since I don't see him around, start shoveling!
jokes.setup: Bart questioning if taking the sugar is stealing
jokes.punchline: Homer's elaborate fake legal justification
jokes.explanation: Excellent Homer logic - completely made-up law delivered with total confidence, plus self-aware village idiot reference

jokes.text: You could've been nicer to Principal Skinner, if you know what I mean. Lisa! I am nice.
jokes.setup: Discussion about Lisa not being skipped ahead
jokes.punchline: Homer's inappropriate suggestion and Marge's innocent misunderstanding
jokes.explanation: Good character contrast - Homer's crude suggestion vs Marge's innocent interpretation

jokes.text: That's because I've loaded it with sugar! But the store sells sugar for 35 cents a pound. And it doesn't have nails and broken glass in it. Those are prizes.
jokes.setup: Family complaining about Homer's cooking tasting strange
jokes.punchline: Homer's deranged enthusiasm for contaminated sugar as 'prizes'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - spinning obvious drawbacks as benefits with complete sincerity

jokes.text: A blasting cap.
jokes.setup: Homer claiming debris in sugar are 'prizes'
jokes.punchline: Lisa's flat delivery of finding actual explosives
jokes.explanation: Great escalation and perfect deadpan delivery from Lisa

jokes.text: My lute!
jokes.setup: Band auditions with normal instruments
jokes.punchline: Student plays medieval instrument
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag - unexpected anachronistic instrument in school band

jokes.text: That was a close one, Lisa, but you made it. I won first chair? No, you regained consciousness. Allison got first chair.
jokes.setup: Lisa hoping she won despite fainting
jokes.punchline: Teacher clarifies she only survived, didn't win
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection - Lisa's hopeful question gets brutally deflated by literal interpretation

jokes.text: Oh, it was just a dream. That was a close one, Lisa, but you made it. I won first chair? No, you regained consciousness. Allison got first chair. And believe me, this is not a dream!
jokes.setup: Lisa hoping the previous scene was a dream
jokes.punchline: Exact same conversation repeats with emphatic reality confirmation
jokes.explanation: Good callback that doubles down on Lisa's misery with the 'not a dream' emphasis

jokes.text: Hello! First born within earshot.
jokes.setup: Homer saying Lisa is his number one
jokes.punchline: Bart's sarcastic reminder of birth order
jokes.explanation: Good Bart moment - perfectly timed sibling jealousy delivered with characteristic snark

jokes.text: I thought I had the tallest hair, but that trip to Graceland really opened my eyes.
jokes.setup: Marge explaining there's always someone better
jokes.punchline: Her personal example about hair height at Elvis's mansion
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge moment - her unique perspective and signature hair obsession

jokes.text: You're thinking of bears, Mom.
jokes.setup: Marge saying Allison is more scared of Lisa than vice versa
jokes.punchline: Lisa's matter-of-fact correction about wildlife advice
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Lisa line - perfectly deadpan correction that reveals Marge's confused maternal advice

jokes.text: Thanks a lot, Simpson, now I'm grounded.
jokes.setup: Homer selling sugar door-to-door to Ned's mother
jokes.punchline: Adult Ned getting grounded by his mother
jokes.explanation: Good character humor - adult Ned still dominated by his mother

jokes.text: That used to be my face in the mud.
jokes.setup: Watching Allison get bullied
jokes.punchline: Lisa's rueful recognition of losing her former status
jokes.explanation: Good character insight - Lisa recognizing her displaced social position

jokes.text: Unless it's from a rubber spider down your dress. That gives me an idea. Note for later: Put rubber spider down Lisa's dress.
jokes.setup: Bart saying he can't stand to see Lisa miserable
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicts himself with prank planning
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart - fake sympathy immediately undercut by gleeful prank planning

jokes.text: Remember how I got Milhouse on America's Most Wanted? There he is on the monkey bars. Try to take him alive. Oh, no! Not again!
jokes.setup: Bart offering to dig up dirt on Allison
jokes.punchline: Completely disproportionate federal response to elementary school tip
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation - innocent playground becomes crime scene, Milhouse's 'not again' suggests pattern

jokes.text: Don't need it. You live in the room next to me. Note: Next year, order fewer cards.
jokes.setup: Bart offering business card for his services
jokes.punchline: Lisa's practical observation and Bart's business self-assessment
jokes.explanation: Good sibling dynamic and Bart's entrepreneurial delusions

jokes.text: While you were out 'earning' that dollar, you lost 40 dollars by not going to work. The plant said if you don't come tomorrow, don't bother coming Monday. Four-day weekend!
jokes.setup: Marge explaining Homer's lost opportunity cost
jokes.punchline: Homer's oblivious celebration of being fired
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - completely misses the point and finds silver lining in disaster

jokes.text: And now Avis Rent A Car is proud to present the second-best band in America. We welcome Garfunkel, Messina, Oates and Lisa, singing their number two hit, 'Born to Runner-Up.'
jokes.setup: Lisa's anxiety about being second best
jokes.punchline: Elaborate nightmare scenario about eternal second place
jokes.explanation: Clever nightmare sequence with great wordplay and cultural references to famous 'second' partners

jokes.text: But I did tip off the feds as to the whereabouts of our good friend Milhouse. But I'm telling you, I didn't do anything. I don't care.
jokes.setup: Bart unable to find dirt on Allison
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of getting Milhouse arrested again
jokes.explanation: Good callback that establishes this as a running gag rather than one-off

jokes.text: Genuine class. Very good. Jeremy Irons. Jeremy's Iron. Well, that's very good for a first try.
jokes.setup: Intellectual family playing anagram games
jokes.punchline: Lisa's weak attempt and condescending response
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment - Lisa's intellectual inadequacy in sophisticated environment

jokes.text: You know, I have a ball. Perhaps you'd like to bounce it.
jokes.setup: Lisa struggling to fit in intellectually
jokes.punchline: Allison's patronizing offer of childish activity
jokes.explanation: Perfect condescending moment - treats Lisa like a much younger child

jokes.text: And, look, I used an old metronome to simulate the heartbeat that drove the killer insane. Neat, huh?
jokes.setup: Allison showing off elaborate diorama
jokes.punchline: Cheerful presentation of murder scene with sound effects
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor - innocent enthusiasm for macabre literary scene

jokes.text: Must protect sugar. Thieves everywhere. The strong must protect the sweet. The sweet.
jokes.setup: Homer becoming paranoid about his sugar stash
jokes.punchline: Philosophical rambling dissolving into obsession
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Homer's sugar obsession into paranoid philosophy

jokes.text: In America, first you get the sugar, then you get the power, then you get the women.
jokes.setup: Homer's sugar empire delusions of grandeur
jokes.punchline: Scarface parody applied to household sweetener
jokes.explanation: Excellent parody - perfectly captures Homer's grandiose delusions while referencing classic film

jokes.text: I nipped it when you let your guard down for that split second. And I'd do it again.
jokes.setup: Homer's paranoia about sugar theft
jokes.punchline: Marge's deadpan confession to 'theft' for tea
jokes.explanation: Good Marge moment - plays along with Homer's paranoia while being practical

jokes.text: I want it all. The terrifying lows, the dizzying highs, the creamy middles. Sure, I might offend a few of the blue noses with my cocky stride and musky odors.
jokes.setup: Homer defending his sugar business
jokes.punchline: Grandiose speech about life philosophy applied to sugar selling
jokes.explanation: Great Homer speech - perfectly captures his delusions of being a rebel entrepreneur

jokes.text: Oh, they're defending themselves somehow.
jokes.setup: Homer being attacked by bees attracted to sugar
jokes.punchline: Casual observation about obvious bee behavior
jokes.explanation: Good understated reaction to obvious situation - classic Homer obliviousness

jokes.text: Is it okay? Well... The important thing is that we survived.
jokes.setup: Lisa's diorama destroyed in accident
jokes.punchline: Bart's attempt at optimism about total destruction
jokes.explanation: Decent sibling support moment but relatively predictable

jokes.text: Then welcome to the nether regions of the soul.
jokes.setup: Lisa agreeing to underhanded tactics
jokes.punchline: Bart's melodramatic philosophical response
jokes.explanation: Great Bart line - unexpected philosophical depth for elementary school scheming

jokes.text: Relatively? There's bound to be some splashback.
jokes.setup: Bart's hose plan promising they'll stay 'relatively' dry
jokes.punchline: Technical analysis of hose logistics
jokes.explanation: Good Bart moment showing his practical approach to mayhem

jokes.text: Enough with the hose!
jokes.setup: Bart repeatedly returning to hose-soaking plan
jokes.punchline: Lisa's exasperated outburst
jokes.explanation: Good escalation payoff - Lisa's frustration with Bart's single-minded focus

jokes.text: Yes, a little too quiet if you know what I mean. I'm afraid I don't. You see, bees usually make a lot of noise. No noise suggests no bees.
jokes.setup: Professional beekeeper noting unusual quiet
jokes.punchline: Homer's complete ignorance of basic bee behavior
jokes.explanation: Good character contrast - professional expertise vs Homer's cluelessness

jokes.text: Very clever, Simpson. Luring our bees to your sugar and selling them back at an inflated price. Bees are on the what, now?
jokes.setup: Beekeeper assuming Homer's elaborate scheme
jokes.punchline: Homer's genuine confusion about bees being on sugar
jokes.explanation: Excellent Homer moment - gets credit for genius scheme he's completely unaware of

jokes.text: Melting! Oh, what a world!
jokes.setup: Homer's sugar empire dissolving in rain
jokes.punchline: Wizard of Oz reference to melting witch
jokes.explanation: Good pop culture reference that fits the moment of Homer's 'empire' dissolving

jokes.text: It's clear now why God portions it out in those tiny packets and why he lives on a plantation in Hawaii.
jokes.setup: Homer learning his lesson about too much sugar
jokes.punchline: Completely wrong theological/geographical conclusion
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - draws completely wrong lesson and imagines God as sugar plantation owner

jokes.text: Here's the grapes, and here's the wrath!
jokes.setup: Student presenting Grapes of Wrath diorama
jokes.punchline: Literal interpretation with actual grapes and angry face
jokes.explanation: Simple visual pun based on misunderstanding literary title

jokes.text: I begged you to look at mine first. I begged you!
jokes.setup: Foreign exchange student's chocolate factory diorama is empty box
jokes.punchline: Desperate plea reveals he ate the chocolate
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Uter's weakness for sweets destroys his project

jokes.text: Hey, everybody! Whoa! Look at me! I'm over here! Turn this way right now! Hey, it's Bart! And he's doing stuff! Oh, I can't take my eyes off him. Look what he's doing.
jokes.setup: Bart creating diversion for Lisa's sabotage
jokes.punchline: Overly obvious attention-grabbing that somehow works perfectly
jokes.explanation: Good meta-humor about obvious diversion tactics somehow working

jokes.text: It's a cow's heart. Them trying to make a monkey out of you.
jokes.setup: Allison's diorama replaced with cow heart
jokes.punchline: Skinner's interpretation as intentional insult
jokes.explanation: Good Skinner moment - his tendency to see conspiracies and disrespect

jokes.text: It's the beating of that hideous heart! I mean, I think I hear something.
jokes.setup: Lisa's guilt over sabotaging Allison
jokes.punchline: Tell-Tale Heart reference reveals her conscience
jokes.explanation: Excellent literary callback that perfectly captures Lisa's guilt - she becomes the narrator of the story she sabotaged

jokes.text: Little sterile. No real insight. What do you think, Miss Hoover? This has been a very disappointing day.
jokes.setup: Judging restored elaborate diorama
jokes.punchline: Harsh criticism of obviously excellent work
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing the teachers' impossible standards and bad mood

jokes.text: Prepackaged Star Wars characters still in their display box? Are those limited edition figures? What's a diorama?
jokes.setup: Ralph's entry that completely misses the assignment
jokes.punchline: Ralph doesn't even know what he was supposed to make
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ralph moment - completely oblivious to the assignment but accidentally wins

jokes.text: I bent my Wookiee.
jokes.setup: Ralph celebrating his victory
jokes.punchline: His only concern is minor damage to toy
jokes.explanation: Classic Ralph line - completely missing the significance of winning, focused on toy damage

jokes.text: My cat's breath smells like cat food.
jokes.setup: Lisa and Allison explaining anagrams
jokes.punchline: Ralph's completely unrelated observation
jokes.explanation: Another perfect Ralph non-sequitur that became legendary - innocent observation delivered with complete sincerity

tmdb_overview: Lisa feels threatened when a new girl moves to Springfield: Allison is smart, pretty, a great saxophone player and a crack diorama builder. Lisa tries to befriend her new rival, but she's consumed with envy and competitiveness.
insight: Lisa's competitive fury drives 57 jokes in Season 6's sharpest character study.
